transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: Hey Alex, thanks for reaching out. I've been drowning in manual data entry for the past three months, so I'm excited to chat about what you guys do.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: Jordan, really appreciate you making time. I saw TechFlow just hit 40 employees — congrats on the growth. So tell me, what's the biggest bottleneck right now when it comes to your data workflows?

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: Honestly? Our ops team is spending probably 15 hours a week just copying and pasting between Salesforce, Slack, and our ERP. We hired two people last quarter just to handle the backlog. It's killing us.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: That's a classic symptoms of scale. When companies hit your size, the tools that got them there start becoming the bottleneck. So you've got Salesforce, Slack, and your ERP — what does your ops team look like today?

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: We've got a core ops team of six people, but two of them are basically just doing data entry. We've talked about hiring one more, but the market for good ops people is tough right now. Plus even if we hire, the problem doesn't really go away, right? We're still doing the same repetitive work.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: Exactly right. Hiring to solve a process problem is like buying more chairs when your table is too small. So when you say the data entry backlog is slowing you down — what specifically suffers? Reporting? Customer experience? Decision-making?

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: All of the above, honestly. Our weekly board deck is always late because someone has to pull the numbers manually. And we had a customer last month who didn't get onboarded for two weeks because the new account data never made it from sales to ops. We lost two weeks of revenue on that one deal.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: That customer onboarding lag is expensive. So when you're thinking about solving this — and correct me if I'm wrong — you're probably looking at this as either a point solution for the Salesforce-to-ERP sync, or you're thinking bigger about automating your entire ops workflow. Which camp are you in?

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: Honestly, at first I just wanted to fix the Salesforce problem. But the more I looked into it, the more I realized the real issue is that we have data everywhere and no one knows what's true. We have three different places where customer data lives. I want one source of truth.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: That's a much more powerful vision. One source of truth changes how decisions get made. So when you say you want to get there — what's your timeline? Are you under pressure to solve this by end of quarter, or is this more of a H1

current_reality.evidence: Ops team 6 people, 2 doing data entry, Salesforce+Slack+ERP, 3 data sources, "one source of truth" vision.

pain_points.evidence: $0 revenue lost from 2-week onboarding lag, board deck always late, hiring 2 people for repetitive work.

decision_process.evidence: Jordan recommendation + budget authority, CFO needs ROI proof, ops leads involved in Thursday deep dive.

budget_authority.evidence: CFO asking questions, hiring cost as baseline, Q2 pressure. Jordan has authority but needs ROI case.

stakes.evidence: Q2 POC goal, CFO pressure, hiring costs, lost revenue. "Results in weeks not quarters" is the bar.

competition.evidence: Zapier outgrown, Workato too expensive, DataSync referral, "build it yourself" resonates.

next_steps.evidence: Thursday 2pm PT deep dive with ops leads. Jordan's hesitation: implementation speed.

nudgeHistory.message: "Who else is involved in decisions like this at TechFlow?"
nudgeHistory.rationale: You have Jordan's perspective but no visibility into how other stakeholders view the problem.

nudgeHistory.message: "What would the ops team's first week with us look like?"
nudgeHistory.rationale: Jordan's concern is implementation time. Concrete timeline details will help close the CFO ROI question.
